title: Poikilospermum peltatum
intro: Poikilospermum peltatum, commonly known as the 'Peltate Polyspermum', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Poikilospermum peltatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Poikilospermum peltatum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Poikilospermum peltatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to New Guinea and nearby islands. The classification of Poikilospermum peltatum is further supported by its placement within the order Malpighiales. This plant is a member of the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a diverse group of plants found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Poikilospermum peltatum has been studied and confirmed through various botanical research and studies.
